subject: Ecology
title: Coral Bleaching
description: A micro-lesson that makes Coral Bleaching usable.

hook.text: When ocean water warms just 1–2°C above normal, corals begin to die in plain sight.

buildup.text: Heat stress forces corals to expel their symbiotic algae, turning the reef ghostly white.

discovery.text: Without algae, corals lose their food source. If heat persists, they starve and die.

twist.text: Mass bleaching events were almost unknown before 1980. Now they happen every few years.

climax.text: The Great Barrier Reef has suffered five mass bleaching events since 2016 alone.

punchline.text: White coral isn't beautiful. It's dying.

quiz.question: What causes coral bleaching?
options: Warm water forces corals to expel symbiotic algae
